title: Genesis: Cain, Abel, and Early Humanity
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of genesis: cain, abel, and early humanity and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study genesis: cain, abel, and early humanity and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What mark did God place on Cain and what was its purpose? Answer: God placed a mark on Cain to protect him from being killed by anyone who found him.
segments.correct_answer: a mark to protect him from being killed
acceptable_variants: a protective mark so no one would kill him
god marked cain for his protection
a sign of protection from vengeance

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: A record of descent from ancestors; Genesis 5 lists the line from Adam to Noah, emphasizing God's covenant continuity through chosen descendants
God's general favor shown to all humanity, including those outside the covenant, as seen in Cain receiving protection and civilization developing in his line
